slides.title: The Global Economy: 2025 & 2026 Strategic Outlook
slides.subtitle: Stabilized growth · AI-driven disruption · Climate as operational risk
slides.notes: Set context: post-volatility stabilization phase. Three macro forces shape 2026 — rate normalization, tariff permanence, climate balance-sheet impact.

slides.title: 01 — Macroeconomic Overview
slides.notes: 5 minutes. Goal: shared baseline on where the global economy is heading.

slides.title: Real GDP Growth Forecast — % YoY
slides.subtitle: 2025E vs 2026F · IMF & Omnicom Economics baseline
slides.notes: Stabilization story: global growth holds; Euro Area recovering; China in managed deceleration. US resilience despite fiscal tightening.

slides.title: Inflation & Monetary Policy Outlook
bullets: Global CPI falls to 3.1% by 2026; US core stays sticky on tariff and labor pressure
Fed targeting 3.0–3.25% neutral range by mid-2026; ECB and Bank of England following
Rate normalization unlocks credit and capex — key tailwind for H2 2026 ad recovery
Disinflation tailwind for late-2026 spending; monitor tariff re-acceleration risk
slides.notes: Emphasis: rate normalization is the hidden tailwind for our business. Slower rates = looser CMO budgets.

slides.title: AI as the New Front Door
bullets: AI agents now control discovery journeys — from product search to purchase decision
Marketing must shift: Search Engine Optimization → AI Agent Optimization (AIAO)
Early LLM signal embedders capture disproportionate share-of-voice in AI-mediated discovery
Omni OS 3.0 is the AI infrastructure layer for 5,000+ client brands
slides.notes: This is the single most important structural shift for our industry. Spend time here.

slides.title: Tariffs: Permanent Design Constraint vs Temporary Shock
bullets_left: Tariffs as temporary disruptions to manage around
Supply chains unchanged; absorb costs short-term
M&A driven by geography and scale
bullets_right: Tariffs as permanent operational design parameters
'K-shaped' M&A: strength concentrated in AI-intensive assets
slides.notes: Key message: clients who still treat tariffs as temporary will be uncompetitive by 2027.

slides.title: Consumer Value Sensitivity in 2026
bullets: 2026 consumers reward utility and transparency — value sensitivity over price alone
Retail Media Networks: $170B+ market linking ad spend to verified transaction outcomes
Brand trust commands 8–12% price premiums — ESG authenticity is now a P&L asset
DTC and loyalty programs are the primary hedge against AI agent disintermediation

slides.title: Climate as an Immediate Balance Sheet Line Item
bullets: 2025 extreme weather disrupted food and energy logistics — insurance premiums up 18%
2026: coastal asset valuations rewritten to reflect escalating climate-driven costs
Climate-stable multi-sourcing now standard; adds 10–15% procurement cost premium
Most-exposed client sectors: FMCG, Travel, Retail, Automotive — core Omnicom revenue

slides.title: Omnicom Revenue Mix — 2026 Projection (%)
slides.subtitle: Precision Marketing and Specialized Services gaining share of total revenue
slides.notes: Data-driven services growing as a share of the mix — reinforcing the platform transformation thesis.

slides.title: Three Strategic Pillars for 2026
bullets: Omni OS 3.0: Unified AI system for creative, media planning, and audience intelligence
Flywheel Digital: Retail media layer linking ad spend to transactions on Amazon, Walmart+
80,000+ employees certified in AI workflows — targeting 15–25% productivity gain

slides.title: SWOT: Marketing & Omnicom in 2026
bullets_left: Disinflation tailwind for consumer spending in late 2026
Retail Media Networks: $170B+ market with clear Omnicom positioning
Omni platform: best-in-class first-party data integration
bullets_right: High global debt levels and potential labor market cooling
Privacy-driven signal loss: third-party cookie deprecation complete
Organizational complexity at global scale constrains agility
Advertising cycle sensitivity — revenue falls faster than costs in downturns
slides.notes: Balanced view. The opportunity set is real — but only if we execute on AI-native transformation before competitors close the gap.

slides.quote: Success in 2026 requires Radical Agility — using AI to compress the distance between market insights and creative execution while building supply chains resilient to geopolitical and climate-driven shocks.
slides.notes: Close on this. Leave it on screen during Q&A.
